ORDER (PURSUANT TO STIPULATION) SHORTENING TIME ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF OMAR HUERTA (MAY 17, 2002, DEPT. 301)

LAW AND MOTION 301, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF OMAR HUERTA. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED VIA COURTCALL. ARGUED AND THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S CCP 473 MOTION ON THE GROUND THAT THERE IS NO EXECUSABLE NEGLECT. HOWEVER, THE COURT DETERMINED THAT OMAR HUERTA OF SAN JOSE SHOULD ANSWER AND SHOULD NOT BE ALLOWED TO TAKE ADVANTAGE OF THE INITIAL MISTAKE MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF WHO SERVED OMAR HUERTA OF SAN PABLO. THE DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE ON FILE SHALL APPLY TO OMAR HUERTA OF SAN PABLO AND THE CLERK SHALL TAKE ANY SUCCESSIVE FILINGS FROM OMAR HUERTA ON SAN JOSE. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTER: ERIC THRONE CSR# 7855
ARBITRATOR PATRICK JOSEPH BYRNE APPOINTED. ARBITRATOR'S JURISDICTION JUN-10-2002 THROUGH SEP-08-2002.

Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Without Prejudice Of Omar Huerta.
Off Calendar May-17-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
C O U R T C A L L A P P E A R A N C E S:
Stephen Terrell, Esq. of ENGSTROM LIPSCOMB & LACK 310/552-3800 for Plaintiff moving to set aside dismissal with prejudice
Gregory M. Heuser, Esq. of HARRIS & GREEN 310/665-8656 for defendant VALLEY RELOCATION AND STORAGE and OMAR HUERTA opposing party

Argued and the Court denied Plaintiff's CCP 473 motion on the ground that there is no execusable neglect. However, the Court determined that OMAR HUERTA of San Jose should answer and should not be allowed to take advantage of the initial mistake made by the Plaintiff who served OMAR HUERTA of San Pablo. The Dismissal With Prejudice on file shall apply to OMAR HUERTA of San Pablo and the Clerk shall take any successive filings from OMAR HUERTA on San Jose. (JJM/cl) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855
